g.data-narration: Welcome to Elliptic Curve Cryptography - a modern public-key system offering strong security with smaller keys.
ECC is a public-key system using elliptic curve mathematics. It provides equivalent security to RSA with much smaller key sizes - just 256 bits versus 3072 bits.
g.text: Modern public-key cryptographic system using algebraic structure of elliptic curves
over finite fields. Provides equivalent security to RSA with significantly smaller keys.

g.data-narration: Compare key sizes: 160-bit ECC equals 1024-bit RSA security. This efficiency makes ECC perfect for mobile, IoT, and blockchain applications.
An elliptic curve over prime field GF(p) follows y² ≡ x³ + ax + b (mod p). The non-singularity condition 4a³ + 27b² ≢ 0 ensures valid curve structure.
Here's the visual representation of an elliptic curve showing its characteristic symmetric shape. Notice how the curve is symmetric about the x-axis.
The group law defines how to add two points on the elliptic curve. Point addition uses the chord method: draw line through P and Q, find third intersection.
g.text: x₃ = m² - x₁ - x₂ mod p
y₃ = m(x₁ - x₃) - y₁ mod p

g.data-narration: Point doubling occurs when P = Q. We use the tangent method: find tangent line at P, the point where it intersects the curve again is 2P.
g.text: y₃ = m(x₁ - x₃) - y₁ mod p

g.data-narration: Scalar multiplication kP adds point P to itself k times. It's the foundation of key generation. Efficient methods use repeated doubling and addition.
g.text: Scalar Multiplication: kP = P + P + ... + P (k times)

g.data-narration: The Elliptic Curve Discrete Logarithm Problem (ECDLP) is ECC's security foundation. Given points P and Q = kP, finding k is computationally infeasible.
g.text: Given: Points P and Q = kP

g.data-narration: Key generation in ECC: choose random private key d, compute public key Q = d × G. The private key is a number, the public key is a point on the curve.
ECDSA signature generation: hash the message, compute r from random kG, calculate s = k⁻¹(e + dr) mod n. The signature is the pair (r, s).
To verify: compute w = s⁻¹, u₁ = ew, u₂ = rw, then point P = u₁G + u₂Q. Accept if r ≡ x₁ mod n.
ECIES (Elliptic Curve Integrated Encryption Scheme): generate ephemeral key, derive shared secret, encrypt message, add MAC for integrity.
ECC is used everywhere: TLS 1.3 for secure web, Bitcoin for transactions, IoT devices for compact security, and government systems.
Key takeaways: ECC provides equivalent security to RSA with smaller keys, uses point arithmetic over finite fields, and powers modern cryptography.
